title: Dolianthus clemensiae
taxonomy: Dolianthus clemensiae belongs to the family Apocynaceae. This family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. The genus Dolianthus is a part of this family and is native to tropical regions of Asia. It is a relatively small genus with a few species, all of which are flowering plants. Dolianthus clemensiae, specifically, is a species within this genus and is known for its unique characteristics and adaptations.

intro: Dolianthus clemensiae, also known as the Clemens' dolianthus, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antimicrobial properties. Its bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Dolianthus clemensiae include decoctions, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat fever, pain, and respiratory infections.
